stories.headline: Meshy Raises Nearly $400M at a $1.5B Valuation, the Largest AI-3D Round Yet
stories.key_takeaway: Meshy's nearly $400M Series B at a $1.5B valuation is the largest AI-3D round to date and signals that 3D generation has become a well-funded, commercially real front in the generative-AI race.

pull_quote.text: Work that once took specialized skills, expensive software, and weeks now takes about a minute and a dollar.

stories.article_excerpt: For most of the generative-AI boom, 3D was the corner of the field that investors admired but rarely bankrolled at scale.

stories.headline: Humanoid Becomes Europe's Newest Robotics Unicorn With a €133M Round
stories.key_takeaway: London's Humanoid became Europe's first pure-play humanoid robotics unicorn with a €133M Series A, but its billion-dollar valuation rests on self-reported pre-orders and pilots rather than shipped commercial units.

pull_quote.text: In just two years, we've gone from an idea to becoming Europe's first pure-play humanoid robotics unicorn, partnered with some of the world's leading industrial companies and built one of the strongest pipelines in the industry.

stories.article_excerpt: London's Humanoid raised €133M at a €1.1B valuation to become Europe's first pure-play humanoid robotics unicorn.

stories.headline: Moonshot AI Seeks a $50B Valuation Ahead of a Hong Kong IPO
stories.key_takeaway: On the strength of its top-ranked open-weight Kimi K3 model, Moonshot AI is seeking a $50 billion valuation ahead of a Hong Kong IPO within six months.

pull_quote.text: Moonshot AI is going toe to toe with Anthropic and OpenAI with far, far fewer resources. It is clearly the strongest open model ever released.

stories.article_excerpt: Beijing's Moonshot AI is in talks to raise at up to $50B ahead of a Hong Kong IPO, powered by its top-ranked Kimi K3 model.

stories.headline: Microsoft Commits $10B to Build Out AI in Japan
stories.key_takeaway: Microsoft's $10 billion, four-year Japan commitment bets that sovereign AI infrastructure — compute, security, and talent kept on national terms — is the new competitive battleground in the global cloud race.

pull_quote.text: We are bringing the world's best technology, building secure and reliable infrastructure on Japan's terms, and helping equip its workforce to accelerate productivity and innovation across its economy.

stories.article_excerpt: Microsoft will pour $10B into Japan through 2029, betting sovereign AI infrastructure is the next competitive moat.

stories.headline: Google Ships Three Gemini Flash Models — but Still No 3.5 Pro
stories.key_takeaway: Google shipped a cheaper, faster, more token-efficient Flash tier to win the high-volume market it can dominate, while its delayed flagship Gemini 3.5 Pro remains conspicuously absent.

pull_quote.text: Google is compressing pricing where it captures volume and holding capability where it captures prestige.

stories.article_excerpt: Google shipped three Gemini Flash models on July 21 — cheaper, faster, more efficient — but the delayed 3.5 Pro was a no-show.

stories.headline: Google Says It's Begun Its Most Ambitious Pretraining Run Yet, for Gemini 4
stories.key_takeaway: Google announced its most ambitious pretraining run yet for Gemini 4 in the same breath as conceding the delayed Gemini 3.5 Pro still isn't shipping, asking the market to look past a stalled flagship.

pull_quote.text: We are currently testing Gemini 3.5 Pro with partners and hope to land soon, and the team has started its most ambitious pre-training run yet for Gemini 4.

stories.article_excerpt: Google concedes Gemini 3.5 Pro is still late while touting its most ambitious pretraining run yet for Gemini 4.

stories.headline: Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber Turns AI Security Into a Three-Way Race
stories.key_takeaway: Google's decision to gate Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber to governments and trusted partners marks a rare industry convergence on restraint that may not survive improving open-weight models.

pull_quote.text: We believe that the defensive benefits of giving capable defenders access to a frontier model significantly outweigh the offensive risks of withholding it, but only if access is gated, audited, and paired with disclosure obligations.

data_points.label: Payloads in Sysdig's autonomous ransomware test

stories.article_excerpt: Google gated Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber to governments and partners, joining Anthropic and Microsoft in a rare convergence on restraint.

stories.headline: Claude Cowork Turns Screen Recordings Into Reusable Skills
stories.key_takeaway: Claude Cowork can now turn a narrated screen recording into a reusable AI skill, replacing prompt-writing with learning by demonstration to unlock the undocumented workplace tasks that stall enterprise AI adoption.

pull_quote.text: It's a little like training a new coworker — except this one never forgets the workflow once it's learned.

stories.article_excerpt: Anthropic's new Cowork feature lets users record and narrate a task once, then have Claude turn it into a reusable skill.

stories.headline: Block Launches Buzz, an Open Workspace Where AI Agents Are First-Class
stories.key_takeaway: Block's Buzz is an open-source, Nostr-based workspace that gives AI agents their own cryptographic identities and treats them as first-class participants alongside humans, challenging the proprietary model of Slack and GitHub.

pull_quote.text: Every company is going to need a place where humans and agents work together. The question is whether that place is proprietary or open.

stories.article_excerpt: Block launched Buzz, an open-source Nostr workspace where AI agents get their own cryptographic identities and sign their own work.

stories.headline: MIT Gives Chip-Based Lidar a Wider View Without Any Moving Parts
stories.key_takeaway: MIT engineers redesigned a silicon-photonics lidar chip's antennas into three different shapes to slash crosstalk from roughly 100% to about 1%, letting solid-state lidar scan a wider, cleaner field of view with no moving parts.

pull_quote.text: The functionality we demonstrated in this work solves a fundamental problem for integrated optical-phased-array technology, enabling future lidar sensors that can achieve significantly higher performance than we could demonstrate previously.

stories.article_excerpt: MIT engineers redesigned a lidar chip's antennas into different shapes to widen its field of view with no moving parts.

stories.headline: Substack Adds AI Detection Just as Research Documents Its Limits
stories.key_takeaway: Substack launched Pangram-powered AI detection the same week Epoch AI documented that such detectors miss up to 18% of AI text imitating a real author's style, underscoring why detection scores shouldn't drive consequences.

pull_quote.text: When we gave models five samples of a specific author's work and asked them to mimic it, an average of 38 of 297 of the resulting passages went undetected.

stories.article_excerpt: Substack rolled out Pangram AI detection the same week Epoch AI showed detectors miss up to 18% of style-imitated text.

stories.headline: A New Study Says Self-Correction, Not Size, Is What Makes Reasoning Models Work
stories.key_takeaway: A widely-circulated July 2026 preprint argues that a reasoning model's ability to catch and fix its own errors mid-chain, not its size or chain length, is the strongest predictor of success on hard math and logic problems.

pull_quote.text: The gains people attribute to size are, to a large degree, gains from correction. Hold the ability to detect and revise errors constant, and the advantage of a bigger model shrinks dramatically.

stories.article_excerpt: A July preprint says self-correction, not model size or chain length, is what makes reasoning models succeed.

stories.headline: AI Lobbying Spending Hits Record Levels in the Second Quarter
stories.key_takeaway: Frontier AI labs poured record sums into federal lobbying in Q2 2026, with Anthropic's $1.97M outspending Nvidia and nearing Oracle — a leading indicator that binding AI regulation is getting real.

pull_quote.text: When a company's lobbying line item doubles year over year, that is not a vanity expense — it is a bet that decisions with real consequences are about to be made.

stories.article_excerpt: AI labs poured record sums into Washington in Q2 2026, with Anthropic's $1.97M outspending Nvidia as rules loom.

stories.headline: Meta Says Its AI Moderates Better Than Humans. Users Who Lost Their Accounts Disagree.
stories.key_takeaway: Meta's AI may be more accurate on average than human moderators, but at a scale of billions of users even a small error rate produces vast numbers of wrongful, individually devastating account bans that its automated appeals system cannot fix.
stories.article_excerpt: Meta says its AI moderates better than humans. At the scale of billions, 'better on average' still means countless wrongful bans.

stories.headline: A Federal Judge Dismisses xAI's Trade-Secret Suit Against OpenAI, With Prejudice
stories.key_takeaway: A California federal judge permanently dismissed xAI's trade-secret suit against OpenAI, ruling that routine recruiting of a rival's engineer is not evidence of misappropriation and raising the bar for AI-industry talent litigation.

pull_quote.text: Holding a future employer liable for a new hire's pre-employment conduct — without any claim that the information was actually used — would risk exposing companies to liability whenever they hired someone who had improperly taken files from a prior job.

stories.article_excerpt: A federal judge dismissed xAI's trade-secret suit against OpenAI with prejudice, finding no evidence of knowing misappropriation.
